question.label: Cross-org ownership
question.name: Cross-org decision area — which one would you want to own?
question.description: Turns his stated aspiration into a concrete scope rather than a generic next-level idea.

answer: I also need clearer sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set.

assessment.note: He names missing sponsorship in priority-setting rooms instead of a decision area to own — strong growth blockage signal; also suggests mild strain around confidence/exposure. [THREAD-DEFERRED] clearer sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set.

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Sponsorship picture
new_queue.name: Who is already backing your strategic work, and who is not yet there?

new_queue.label: Next commitment
new_queue.name: What is the one move you want to make before we next meet?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 6)

history.answer_excerpt: I also need clearer sponsorship in rooms where priorities are set.
Sometimes I avoid high-ambiguity forums because I do not want to sound underprepared.
I think I need to own a cross-org decision area, not just deliver strong design outcomes.
I am still motivated, but the promotion outcome made me more careful about where I spend political capital.

I can lead major initiatives, but I do not always translate that into visible strategic influence.
I get positive feedback on craft and collaboration, but less clarity on what separates me at the next level.
